# qlik_app_script (Resource)

Manages the current script version of a Qlik Cloud application.  
Use the `file()` function to load the script text from a local `.qvs` file in your repository.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "qlik_app" "my_app" {
  name        = "My Application"
  description = "An application with a managed script"
}

resource "qlik_app_script" "my_script" {
  app_id           = qlik_app.my_app.id
  script           = file("${path.module}/scripts/main.qvs")
  version_message  = "Initial script version"
}

# Note: scripts/main.qvs is a file maintained in your repository alongside this configuration.
```

## Import

Import an existing app script using the app ID:

    terraform import qlik_app_script.my_script <appId>

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `app_id` (String) The identifier of the parent application.
- `script` (String) The raw script text. Use the `file()` function to load from disk.

### Optional

- `version_message` (String) An optional description of this script version.

### Read-Only

- `script_id` (String) The unique identifier of the script.
- `modified_time` (String) The last modification time of the script version.
- `modifier_id` (String) The identifier of the user who last modified the script.
